steps.title: Read Evan's city reaction
steps.content: The passage shows Evan feeling enthusiasm, envy, and curiosity after moving to New York City. His memory that back home there was little worth going to highlights how strongly the new environment affects him.

steps.title: Reject unsupported purposes
steps.content: Choice B explains why he traveled, which the passage does not do. Choice C invents a comparison of thoughts at different times of day. Choice D says he regrets leaving home, but the passage shows excitement instead. The answer is A.
